issue_title: List of tested database in docs doesn't match pom.xml
issue_body:
### Before reporting an issue

- [X] I have searched existing issues
- [X] I have reproduced the issue with the latest release

### Area

docs

### Describe the bug

The docs in 

https://github.com/keycloak/keycloak/blob/76ee1be76cd20dbffe056a5a342843bf14266125/docs/guides/server/db.adoc#L20-L24

list different database versions as "tested" compared to 

https://github.com/keycloak/keycloak/blob/67c64c37df4c33a90f4db1873620da06fb751cd4/pom.xml#L161-L172



### Version

nightly

### Expected behavior

The docs should be up-to-date. Maybe we could even get this information from the `pom.xml` file so it is always up-to-date.

### Actual behavior

Information in the docs is outdated.

### Anything else?

It would be nice to use the information directly from the pom.xml
commit_message:
Referencing information in pom.xml for the list of tested databases

Closes: #21349

issue_title: Align httpclient dependency with the Quarkus Platform BOM
issue_body:
### Description

httpclient removal caused [unit test failures](https://github.com/keycloak/keycloak/actions/runs/5411587832/jobs/9834779968)

[By reverting the changes](https://github.com/keycloak/keycloak/pull/21078/commits/ed5e9df7ec6af0137131fe6fe71ee362cd4e2667) and re-adding the dependency override back to the parent POM, all the workflows got stable again. I didn't have time to investigate the cause in more depth yet, therefore it's expected to address it in this follow-up as both the dependencies can be possibly aligned with the Quarkus Platform BOM.

This task serves as an follow-up of https://github.com/keycloak/keycloak/pull/21078.

we need to have a dependency on commons-logging-jboss-logging

issue_title: SSSD users with capitals in the email cannot login to keycloak
issue_body:
### Before reporting an issue

- [X] I have searched existing issues
- [X] I have reproduced the issue with the latest release

### Area

core

### Describe the bug

If a user in SSSD has capitals letters in the email (for example `Ricardo.Martin@redhat.com`) the account is imported into keycloak but later the account is always detected as different. The reason is that the email is imported in lowercase and the comparison is always different.

The resulting account cannot be used for login because it always fails with `user_not_found`.

```
WARN  [org.keycloak.events] (executor-thread-7) type=LOGIN_ERROR, realmId=587c66b4-4ccf-4f06-8440-40647475d95c, clientId=account-console, userId=null, ipAddress=192.168.100.1, error=user_not_found, auth_method=openid-connect, auth_type=code, redirect_uri=http://rhidm.sample.com:8081/realms/master/account/#/personal-info, code_id=6053d9e7-afd4-40cd-aaa8-78975f8f6c40, username=emily
```

### Version

main

### Expected behavior

The user can login no matter the email has capitals or not.

### Actual behavior

`user_not_found` error.

### How to Reproduce?

Just create a user in ipa with capitals in the email:

```
ipa user-add emily --first=Emily --last=Jones --email=Emily@jones.com
```

Try to login with that user.


### Anything else?

_No response_
commit_message:
Compare SSSD email ignoring the case
Closes https://github.com/keycloak/keycloak/issues/21394

issue_title: Brute Force Detection is disabled when updating frontenUrl via admin client
issue_body:
### Before reporting an issue

- [X] I have searched existing issues
- [X] I have reproduced the issue with the latest release

### Area

admin/client/node

### Describe the bug

When the frontenUrl is updated through the admin client e.g.: 
```     
 await this.client.realms.update(
        {realm},
        {
          attributes: {
            frontendUrl: `${frontendUrl}/auth`,
          },
        },
      );
 ```

the brute force detection mechanism is unexpectedly disabled.

### Version

21.1.1

### Expected behavior

Changing the frontenUrl attribute should not affect brute force settings

### Actual behavior

Brute force detection is disabled when changing frontenUrl

### How to Reproduce?

1. Enable brute force detection for a realm using the Keycloak admin UI.
2. Set a new frontendUrl for this realm using the Keycloak admin client.
3. Inspect the brute force settings for this realm using the Keycloak admin UI.

### Anything else?

Same behaviour with security header settings. They are also deleted when the frontendUrl is updated. Seems to affect all security defence settings of the realm

issue_title: Cookie error on second browser tab
issue_body:
### Before reporting an issue

- [X] I have searched existing issues
- [X] I have reproduced the issue with the [latest nightly release](https://github.com/keycloak/keycloak/releases/tag/nightly)

### Area

core

### Describe the bug

I open the client URL in my browser and get redirected to the keycloak login page.
I enter my credentials, successfully authenticate and afterwards get redirected to the webpage of my client.

I open a second tab in my browser and in there again open the same client URL.
I get redirected to keycloak, keycloak notices me beeing loged in already and so I get redirected to the webpage of my client.

So far, so good.

Now I log out on that second tab.
My client redirects me to the Keycloak login screen where I can enter my credentials.
What I mean by that is:

- I get redirected to Domain/auth/realms/Realm/protocol/openid-connect/logout
- then I get redirected to the client_url
- then (cause I am not authenticated) I get redirected to Domain/auth/realms/Realm/protocol/openid-connect/auth

After entering my username and password, Keycloak presents me with the error "Cookie not found. Please make sure cookies are enabled in your browser.".
No exception is logged in the keycloak backend.
In the browser an AUTH_SESSION_ID and an AUTH_SESSION_ID_LEGACY cookie are available (the KC_RESTART cookie is missing, but that does not explain the error).

### Version

21.0.2

### Expected behavior

I should be able to properly log out from every browsertab.

### Actual behavior

When logged out from second browser tab, a cookie error is thrown.

### How to Reproduce?

1. Open browser tab 1 and log into keycloak client
2. Open browser tab 2 and navigate to keycloak client (be logged in cause of SSO)
3. Log out on tab 2
4. Relogin on tab 2
--> Cookie error

### Anything else?

_No response_

issue_title: Can create a user without the registration flow finished properly (reopened #17644)
issue_body:
### Before reporting an issue

- [X] I have searched existing issues
- [X] I have reproduced the issue with the [latest nightly release](https://github.com/keycloak/keycloak/releases/tag/nightly)

### Area

storage

### Describe the bug

Reopening #17644 as suggested on [Slack here](https://cloud-native.slack.com/archives/C056XU905S6/p1688643055350429?thread_ts=1688559314.400539&cid=C056XU905S6)

There was a PR (#19488) that stated to solve the issue, but it did not. It can be reproduced with the latest nightly at the moment.

I can create a User entity with the registration flow throwing an Exception. Not all elements are run in the registration flow in such cases, but the user still exists. Neither a REGISTER event is saved for the user.

### Version

latest nightly (but present since at least 19.x)

### Expected behavior

If the user entity is created then the registration flow should execute until the end successfully. Probably the whole flow should run in a single transaction.

### Actual behavior

The user entity is created, but an error page is shown to the user. The registration flow is not executed until the end.

### How to Reproduce?

A realm is required where the following is set:
 - registration enabled
 - forgot password enabled

To reproduce:
 - Open the login page
 - Click on the _register_ link
 - Click on the _back to the login_ link
 - Click on the _forgot password_ link
 - Enter a valid, existing e-mail address to the form. Click on the Submit button
 - Press the back button in the browser until you get back to the registration form (~3 times?)
   - a _page is expired_ error will be shown somewhere. Don't care about it, just press the back button until you get back to the login page
 - Fill out the registration form & submit your registration

An error page is shown, but the user is created in the database. From now on the freshly created user can ask for a reset password link, and log in after that with their newly created password.

### Anything else?

This is a reopen of #17644 as suggested on [Slack here](https://cloud-native.slack.com/archives/C056XU905S6/p1688643055350429?thread_ts=1688559314.400539&cid=C056XU905S6)

There was a PR (#19488) that stated to solve the issue, but it didoes not. It can be reproduced with the latest nightly at the moment.

I have a PR in #19556 which solves the issue, opened months ago.
commit_message:
Registration flow fixed (#23064)

Closes #21514

issue_title: Context path missing in URL on OTP page to switch between QR code and manual code
issue_body:
### Before reporting an issue

- [X] I have searched existing issues
- [X] I have reproduced the issue with the [latest nightly release](https://github.com/keycloak/keycloak/releases/tag/nightly)

### Area

account/ui

### Describe the bug

When Keycloak is publicly exposed on a different path, the link generated for the OTP setup page to switch between QR code and manual code entry does not reflect the public path. Debugging showed that the contextPath provided by the hostname provider is not considered when building the URLs.

Setup:
There is a reverse proxy that is redirecting every request on http://hostname:port/foo/auth on a docker that exposes keycloak on http://ip:port/auth, so public path deviates from http-relative-path of Keycloak, all other functionality works.

### Version

21.1.1

### Expected behavior

The contextPath of hostname provider is respected when generating the URL to switch between QR code and manual code entry for OTP setup.

### Actual behavior

The contextPath of the hostname provider is ignored.

### How to Reproduce?

1 - Use a reverse proxy like apache or nginx
2 - Make your Keycloak run on ip:port/auth (using http-relative-path and set env var KC_HOSTNAME_URL and KC_HOSTNAME_ADMIN_URL to ip:port/foo/auth
3 - Reverse proxy the URL ip:port/foo/auth to your Keycloak that is running ip:port/auth
4 - Go to the OTP configuration page
5 - Try to switch to manual, the url is pointing on /auth and not to /foo/auth

### Anything else?

This is related to https://github.com/keycloak/keycloak/issues/11774 which focues on the ip/hostname and port, but was not using a different path.

issue_title: Make sure admin events are not referencing sensitive data from their representation
issue_body:
### Description

It is a follow-up on https://github.com/keycloak/keycloak/issues/17470.

In addition to removing credentials from user representations when persisting admin events, we should also make sure other representation types are not exposing any sensitive data.

The representation types we should start looking at are:

* `RealmRepresentation`
* `UserRepresentation`
* `ClientRepresentation`
* `IdentityProviderRepresentation`
* `ComponentRepresentation`

Ideally, we should enhance how we are using `StripSecretsUtils` to encapsulate how sensitive data are removed from representations for the different representation types. Therefore, helping to administer how sensitive data, such as secrets, are returned from Admin API and set to event representations.

### Discussion

https://github.com/keycloak/keycloak/issues/17470

### Motivation

Avoid exposing sensitive data when returning representations from Admin API or when creating/persisting events.

### Details

_No response_

Refactored StripSecretsUtils in order to make it unit-testable, added unit tests for it
Don't mask secrets at realm export

issue_title: CVE-2022-45868 H2 Cleartext Storage of Sensitive Information
issue_body:
### Description

**Source**: Trivy
**Location**: opt/keycloak/lib/lib/main/com.h2database.h2-2.1.214.jar
Package: com.h2database:h2
Installed Version: 2.1.214
Vulnerability CVE-2022-45868
Severity: HIGH
Fixed Version: 2.2.220
Link: [CVE-2022-45868](https://avd.aquasec.com/nvd/cve-2022-45868)
The web-based admin console in H2 Database Engine through 2.1.214 can be started via the CLI with the argument -webAdminPassword, which allows the user to specify the password in cleartext for the web admin console. Consequently, a local user (or an attacker that has obtained local access through some means) would be able to discover the password by listing processes and their arguments. NOTE: the vendor states "This is not a vulnerability of H2 Console ... Passwords should never be passed on the command line and every qualified DBA or system administrator is expected to know that."

issue_title: CVE-2023-33201 - Information Exposure vulnerability in org.bouncycastle:bcprov-jdk15on
issue_body:
### Description

Bouncy Castle For Java before 1.74 is affected by an LDAP injection vulnerability. The vulnerability only affects applications that use an LDAP CertStore from Bouncy Castle to validate X.509 certificates. During the certificate validation process, Bouncy Castle inserts the certificate's Subject Name into an LDAP search filter without any escaping, which leads to an LDAP injection vulnerability.

**Source**: Snyk Open Source
**Location**: pom.xml
This file introduces a vulnerable org.bouncycastle:bcprov-jdk15on package with a medium severity vulnerability.
(CVE-2023-33201) org.bouncycastle:bcprov-jdk15on@1.70

* Package Manager: maven
* Vulnerable module: org.bouncycastle:bcprov-jdk15on
* Introduced through: org.keycloak:keycloak-operator@999.0.0-SNAPSHOT and org.bouncycastle:bcprov-jdk15on@1.70
### Detailed paths
* _Introduced through_: org.keycloak:keycloak-operator@999.0.0-SNAPSHOT › org.bouncycastle:bcprov-jdk15on@1.70
# Overview
[org.bouncycastle:bcprov-jdk15on](https://mvnrepository.com/artifact/org.bouncycastle/bcprov-jdk15on) is a Java implementation of cryptographic algorithms.

Affected versions of this package are vulnerable to Information Exposure due to missing validation for the X.500 name of any certificate, subject, or issuer. The presence of a wild card may lead to information disclosure. This could allow a malicious user to obtain unauthorized information via blind LDAP Injection, exploring the environment and enumerating data. 

**Note:**

The exploit depends on the structure of the target LDAP directory as well as what kind of errors are exposed to the user.
# Remediation
A fix was pushed into the `master` branch but not yet published.
# References
- [GitHub Commit](https://github.com/bcgit/bc-java/commit/b00b58873643abc7e834f07e345e0165bb3a1786)
- [GitHub Wiki](https://github.com/bcgit/bc-java/wiki/CVE-2023-33201)
- [RedHat Bugzilla Bug](https://bugzilla.redhat.com/show_bug.cgi?id=2215465)

### Area

operator

### Describe the bug

When using the new Keycloak Operator in combination with flux, and flux is reconciling the manifests, the following error is being produced.
```
Keycloak/keycloak/keycloak dry-run failed: failed to create manager for existing fields: failed to convert new object (keycloak/keycloak; k8s.keycloak.org/v2alpha1, Kind=Keycloak) to smd typed: errors:

           	             	                 	        	    	 .status.conditions[1].status: expected map, got &{False}                                                                                                                                               	
           	             	                 	        	    	 .status.conditions[2].status: expected map, got &{False}   
```

The kube-api-server shows the following logs:
```
I0711 17:36:31.741824      11 store.go:1482] "Monitoring resource count at path" resource="keycloaks.k8s.keycloak.org" path="<storage-prefix>//k8s.keycloak.org/keycloaks"

I0711 17:36:32.380839      11 cacher.go:435] cacher (keycloakrealmimports.k8s.keycloak.org): initialized
I0711 17:38:35.453034      11 cacher.go:779] cacher (controllerrevisions.apps): 1 objects queued in incoming channel.

I0711 17:38:35.453063      11 cacher.go:779] cacher (controllerrevisions.apps): 3 objects queued in incoming channel.
I0711 17:38:35.453068      11 cacher.go:779] cacher (controllerrevisions.apps): 4 objects queued in incoming channel.
E0711 17:38:44.281384      11 fieldmanager.go:210] "[SHOULD NOT HAPPEN] failed to update managedFields" err=<
	failed to convert new object (keycloak/keycloak; k8s.keycloak.org/v2alpha1, Kind=Keycloak) to smd typed: errors:
	 .status.conditions[0].status: expected map, got &{True}
	 .status.conditions[1].status: expected map, got &{False}
	 .status.conditions[2].status: expected map, got &{False}
 > VersionKind="k8s.keycloak.org/v2alpha1, Kind=Keycloak" namespace="keycloak" name="keycloak"
```

I suggest from the `&{True}` that somehow a boolean literal is being read, however my current keycloak manifest (`kubectl get keycloaks.k8s.keycloak.org keycloak -o yaml`) returns strings as status:
```yaml
status:
  ```

issue_title: Have Route created on OCP even when no Keycloak CR hostname specified
issue_body:
### Description

I want to use Keycloak on OpenShift (OCP) and want to make sure there is `Route` created for Keycloak, even if I had not specified upfront the hostname value for Keycloak to use.

Seems that Keycloak operator assumes that `Route` for Keycloak is auto-generated by OpenShift based on the `Ingress` resource creates, granted one specifies `className: openshift-default` in the `Keycloak` CR. And then, when if there is `Keycloak.spec.hostname` specified, [adds](https://github.com/keycloak/keycloak/blob/main/operator/src/main/java/org/keycloak/operator/controllers/KeycloakIngress.java#L111) the `host` into `Ingress` resource created.


However, on OCP 4.12+, [`route-controller-manager`](https://github.com/openshift/route-controller-manager) creates `Route` based on the `Ingress` resource, [ONLY if the `Ingress` resource has `.spec.rules.host` specified](https://github.com/openshift/route-controller-manager/blob/main/pkg/route/ingress/ingress.go#L416), otherwise no `Route` is created.

It is perfectly valid to create `Route` on OpenShift, without `host` being specified - in such case, OCP auto-generated the host in the `*.apps.<cluster-dns-name>` subdomain.

So, my key questions are:
- Is the assumption that `hostname` is specified in `Keycloak` CR correct - i.e. is this parameter absolutely required for Keycloak application to work correctly?
  - what breaks when `hostname` is not specified in `Keycloak` CR?
- If `hostname` parameter is not required, should we have the updated Keycloak operator logic in the appropriate way, so that `Route` is created on OpenShift (perhaps a strict tie to Ingres->Route autogeneration could be revised?)

A bit related discussion: https://github.com/keycloak/keycloak/issues/20128 , since eventually we'd like to use the re-encrypt Routes, with the certifies being rotated and replaced with customer's certificates.

### Discussion

_No response_

### Motivation

Using Keyclok on OpenShift requires `Route` to be exposed outside of the cluster, for external applications / processes interactions.

### Details

_No response_

issue_title: DN with RDN that contains trailing backslash is imported incorrectly into Keycloak
issue_body:
### Before reporting an issue

- [X] I have searched existing issues
- [X] I have reproduced the issue with the [latest nightly release](https://github.com/keycloak/keycloak/releases/tag/nightly)

### Area

ldap

### Describe the bug

When connecting Keycloak to an AD LDAP server with a DN that has a trailing backslash in one of the RDNs, the DN is imported incorrectly. 

AD DN: `CN=Test User\\\\,OU=Users,DC=example,DC=com`

But when imported and viewed in Keycloak it is no longer a valid DN. 

Keycloak LDAP_ENTRY_DN: `CN=Test User\\\\,OU,DC=example,DC=com`

This causes login and parsing issues for the entity that is imported. 

### Version

20.0.2, 21.1.2

### Expected behavior

Expected behavior is that the entity with a DN containing an RDN with the trailing backslash would be imported correctly with the same DN as seen in the AD server. eg `CN=Test User\\\\,OU=Users,DC=example,DC=com`

### Actual behavior

Actual behavior is that the entity with a DN containing an RDN with the trailing backslash is imported incorrectly and the entity cannot be used in Keycloak. eg `CN=Test User\\\\,OU,DC=example,DC=com`

### How to Reproduce?

Install Keycloak and connect to an Active Directory LDAP server that has an entity with a DN containing an RDN with a trailing backslash. eg `CN=Test User\\\\,OU=Users,DC=example,DC=com`

### Anything else?

_No response_

dist/quarkus

When starting Quarkus with a TLS certificate, there are two warnings logged at the console: 

> The 'quarkus.http.ssl.certificate.key-file' config property is deprecated and should not be used anymore
The 'quarkus.http.ssl.certificate.file' config property is deprecated and should not be used anymore

Looking at the issue that change this, this is was part of Quarkus 2, so it is quite old.

### Version

main

There should be no warning during startup. Possibly Keycloak should handle multiple server names as Quarkus does, see https://github.com/quarkusio/quarkus/pull/17036

There are warnings at the start up that confuse/distract/unsettle the user. A user can specify only one server certificate.

### How to Reproduce?

Start Keycloak with a TLS certificate

### Anything else?

There could be multiple steps here, which could be split: 

1. Get rid of the warning by using the new property
2. Support multiple TLS certificates (which might require code and documentation updates)

At least the first part (getting rid of the warning) might be something to backport to KC22

issue_title: Operator doesn't pass on system property 'jgroups.dns.query' to Keycloak but an env variable, leading to a warning in the log
issue_body:
### Before reporting an issue

- [X] I have searched existing issues
- [X] I have reproduced the issue with the [latest nightly release](https://github.com/keycloak/keycloak/releases/tag/nightly)

### Area

operator

### Describe the bug

To configure discovery for Infinispan on Kubernetes, the [docs](https://www.keycloak.org/server/caching) as for the `jgroups.dns.query` to be added as a Java System Property using `-D...`. Instead the Operator passes an environment variable which leads to a warning. 

### Version

main

### Expected behavior

No warning logged.



### Actual behavior

Warning logged by Infinispan, which is misleading and wrong, as it is evaluated by JGroups in a second step:

> ISPN000901: Property jgroups.dns.query could not be replaced as intended!

This is what is happening:

The Keycloak Operator set an environment variable `jgroups.dns.query`: 

https://github.com/keycloak/keycloak/blob/fa830344743c987e71c2d6cf3eea161e51e7c43b/operator/src/main/java/org/keycloak/operator/controllers/KeycloakDeployment.java#L350-L353

The environment variable is not visible inside of a shell, as shells ignore environment variable names which contain a dot. It is still there when I look at /proc/1/environ, and the Java process picks it up as it is started directly, and not from a shell. 

After some debugging with @ryanemerson I found that Infinispan's [`StringPropertyReplacer`](https://docs.jboss.org/infinispan/14.0/apidocs/org/infinispan/commons/util/StringPropertyReplacer.html) doesn't look at environment variables and prints the warning, while JGroup's `ProtocolConfiguration.substituteVariables` calls `Util.substituteVariable()` which then takes into account the environment variables as well.




### How to Reproduce?

Look at the logs of Keycloak started by the Operator in Kubernetes.

### Anything else?

Looking at the JavaDoc of `StringPropertyReplacer`, environment variables need to be prefixed by `env.` to be replaced by Infinispan.

As we're using the default stacks and don't want to duplicate them, we can't use an environment variable. 

The JAVA_OPTS shouldn't be overwritten, as it would overwrite all defaults in `kc.sh`. The `JAVA_OPTS_APPEND` is usually overwritten using the `podTemplate`, so if the Operator wants to use that path, it should only append that option there.

fix: moves jgroups.dns.query to a system property (#24057)

* fix: moves jgroups.dns.query to a system property

closes #21830

also removing cache-stack
